sentences.zh: 太后曰：「新都侯莽前以大司馬奉送先帝大行，曉習故事，吾令莽佐君。

translations.literal: The empress dowager said: "Marquis of Xindu Mang, earlier as grand marshal, escorted the late emperor's catafalque and is thoroughly versed in precedent; I order Mang to assist you.
translations.idiomatic: She said, "Wang Mang of Xindu once ran the late emperor's obsequies as grand marshal; he knows the rites. Let him help you."

sentences.zh: 」賢頓首幸甚。

translations.literal: " Xian knocked his head in gratitude that it was so fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: " Dong Xian kowtowed in relief."

sentences.zh: 太后遣使者召莽。

translations.literal: The empress dowager dispatched envoys to summon Mang.
translations.idiomatic: She sent for Wang Mang at once.

sentences.zh: 既至，以太后指使尚書劾賢帝病不親醫藥，禁止賢不得入出宮殿司馬中。

translations.literal: When he arrived, by the empress dowager's order she had the secretariat impeach Xian for not personally overseeing medicine when the emperor was ill, and forbade Xian from entering or leaving the palace compound.
translations.idiomatic: Once Wang Mang arrived, she had the secretariat charge Dong Xian with neglecting the sick emperor's physic and barred him from the palace gates.

sentences.zh: 賢不知所為，詣闕免冠徒跣謝。

translations.literal: Xian did not know what to do; he went to the palace gate bareheaded and barefoot to apologize.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian stumbled to the gate, capless and shoeless, to beg forgiveness.

sentences.zh: 莽使謁者以太后詔即闕下冊賢曰：「間者以來，陰陽不調，災害並臻，元元蒙辜。

translations.literal: Mang had an usher read the empress dowager's edict at the gate dismissing Xian: "Of late yin and yang have not been in harmony, disasters have arrived together, and the common people suffer wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang had an usher intone a rescript at the foot of the wall: "Heaven and earth are out of joint; calamities pile up and the people bear the cost."

sentences.zh: 夫三公，鼎足之輔也，高安侯賢未更事理，為大司馬不合眾心，非所以折衝綏遠也。

translations.literal: The three excellencies are the tripod's supporting legs; Marquis of Gao'an Xian has not yet learned how affairs are managed; as grand marshal he does not accord with the multitude's heart—this is no way to blunt the foe's edge and quiet the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: The three dukes should steady the state; Marquis of Gao'an Dong Xian is green at government, sits as grand marshal against every expectation, and cannot keep the realm secure."

sentences.zh: 其收大司馬印綬，罷歸第。

translations.literal: Let him surrender the grand marshal's seals and ribbons, be dismissed, and return to his residence.
translations.idiomatic: He shall return the grand marshal's seals and retire to his house."

sentences.zh: 」即日賢與妻皆自殺，家惶恐夜葬。

translations.literal: " That same day Xian and his wife both committed suicide; the household panicked and buried them by night.
translations.idiomatic: " That evening Dong Xian and his wife killed themselves; kin buried them hastily in the dark."

sentences.zh: 莽疑其詐死，有司奏請發賢棺，至獄診視。

translations.literal: Mang suspected a feigned death; the authorities memorialized requesting to open Xian's coffin and bring it to the prison for inspection.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang suspected a ruse and had officials demand that the coffin be opened and the body examined in jail.

sentences.zh: 莽復風大司徒光奏：「賢質性巧佞，翼奸以獲封侯，父子專朝，兄弟並寵，多受賞賜，治第宅，造塚壙，放效無極，不異王制，費以萬萬計，國家為空虛。

translations.literal: Mang also prompted Grand Minister of Education Kong Guang to memorialize: "Xian's nature was crafty and fawning; he winged treachery to win a marquisate; father and son monopolized the court, brothers shared favor together; they received vast gifts, built mansions and tombs, aped the limitless style of kings, not differing from royal institutions, the expense reckoned in the hundred millions and emptying the state.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang then steered Grand Minister Kong Guang into memorializing that Dong Xian had flattered his way to a fief, that father and sons had hijacked the government, that their houses and mortuary parks aped imperial scale at a cost of billions, hollowing the treasury."

sentences.zh: 父子驕蹇，至不為使者禮，受賜不拜，罪惡暴著。

translations.literal: Father and son were arrogant and overbearing to the point of not observing etiquette toward envoys, and receiving gifts without bowing—their crimes were manifest.
translations.idiomatic: They were insolent even to imperial messengers and took gifts without bowing—their guilt was obvious."

sentences.zh: 賢自殺伏辜，死後父恭等不悔過，乃復以沙畫棺四時之色，左蒼龍，右白虎，上著金銀日月，玉衣珠璧以棺，至尊無以加。

translations.literal: Xian killed himself and confessed his guilt; after his death Father Gong and the others did not repent, but again painted the coffin with the colors of the four seasons, azure dragon on the left, white tiger on the right, gold and silver sun and moon above, jade shroud and pearl disks in the coffin—nothing more could be added for the utmost veneration.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian's suicide closed his case, yet Dong Gong lined the coffin with seasonal emblems, azure dragon and white tiger, gold and silver sun and moon, jade suit and pearls—a burial fit for an emperor."

sentences.zh: 恭等幸得免於誅，不宜在中土。

translations.literal: Gong and the others fortunately escaped execution; they should not remain in the central lands.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Gong should not be left in the heartland after such mercy."

sentences.zh: 臣請收沒入財物縣官。

translations.literal: Your servant requests confiscating their property for the exchequer.
translations.idiomatic: We ask that their wealth be forfeited to the treasury."

sentences.zh: 諸以賢為官者皆免。

translations.literal: All who had gained office through Xian were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Everyone who had risen through Dong Xian must be stripped of rank."

sentences.zh: 」父恭、弟寬信與家屬徙合浦，母別歸故郡巨鹿。

translations.literal: " Father Gong, younger brother Kuanxin, and the household were removed to Hepu; the mother was separately returned to her old commandery Julu.
translations.idiomatic: " Dong Gong and Dong Kuanxin went to Hepu with the clan; the mother was sent home to Julu."

sentences.zh: 長安中小民言雚嘩，鄉其第哭，幾獲盜之。

translations.literal: The minor commoners of Chang'an clamored in uproar, flocked to his mansion to weep, and the tumult nearly turned to looting.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an commoners mobbed the Dong gate wailing until the crowd nearly rioted for plunder."

sentences.zh: 縣官斥賣董氏財凡四十三萬萬。

translations.literal: The exchequer sold off Dong clan property for a total of forty-three ten-thousands of ten-thousands in cash.
translations.idiomatic: The state auctioned the Dong estates for some forty-three hundred million cash."

sentences.zh: 賢既見發，裸診其屍，因埋獄中。

translations.literal: When Xian had been exhumed, they stripped the corpse for examination and buried it in the prison.
translations.idiomatic: When the coffin was forced open, the body was stripped for inspection and dumped in the prison yard."

sentences.zh: 賢所厚吏沛硃詡自劾去大司馬府，買棺衣收賢屍葬之。

translations.literal: Zhu Xu of Pei, whom Xian had favored, impeached himself and left the grand marshal's office, bought a coffin and shroud, collected Xian's corpse, and buried it.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Xu of Pei, once a Dong Xian client, resigned from the grand marshal's staff, bought a coffin, and gave Dong Xian a secret burial.

sentences.zh: 王莽聞之而大怒，以它罪擊殺詡。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang heard of it he was greatly furious and on another charge had Xu beaten to death.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang, enraged, found another pretext and had Zhu Xu executed.

sentences.zh: 詡子浮建武中貴顯，至大司馬、司空，封侯。

translations.literal: Xu's son Fu in the Jianwu era rose to eminence, reaching grand marshal and minister of works, and was enfeoffed marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Xu's son Zhu Fu, under Guangwu, climbed to grand marshal, minister of works, and a marquisate.

sentences.zh: 而王閎王莽時為牧守，所居見紀，莽敗乃去官。

translations.literal: Wang Hong during Mang's time served as regional governor; where he governed was recorded in praise; when Mang fell he left office.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong became a provincial governor under Mang and left a good record; he quit when Mang fell.

sentences.zh: 世祖下詔曰：「武王克殷，表商容之閭，閎修善謹敕，兵起，吏民獨不爭其頭首。

translations.literal: Shizu issued an edict saying: "When King Wu conquered Yin, he marked Shang Rong's lane; Hong cultivated goodness and was restrained; when armies rose, clerks and people alone did not vie to take his head.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu decreed: "King Wu of Zhou honored Shang Rong's lane after conquering Shang; Wang Hong lived prudently and well, so that when rebellion broke out no mob tried to kill him for sport."

sentences.zh: 今以閎子補吏。

translations.literal: Now appoint Hong's son to fill a clerkship.
translations.idiomatic: Let his son be given an official post."

sentences.zh: 」至墨綬卒官。

translations.literal: " He reached black-ribbon rank and died in office.
translations.idiomatic: " He rose to magistrate with the black ribbon and died in harness."

sentences.zh: 蕭咸外孫雲。

translations.literal: Fu was the maternal grandson of Xiao Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu was Xiao Xian's grandson on the distaff side.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：柔曼之傾意，非獨女德，蓋亦有男色焉。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: yielding softness that sways the heart is not the virtue of women alone—there is also the power of male beauty.
translations.idiomatic: The summation reads: the spell of soft beauty is not only a woman's weapon; men too have turned rulers' heads with it.

sentences.zh: 觀籍、閎、鄧、韓之徒非一，而董賢之寵尤盛，父子並為公卿，可謂貴重人臣無二矣。

translations.literal: Looking at Ji, Hong, Deng, Han, and their like—not a single case—Dong Xian's favor was especially great; father and son both became ministers; one may say no subject was ever more honored.
translations.idiomatic: From Ji Ru and Hong Ru through Deng Tong and Han Yan, none matched Dong Xian: father and son both sat among the highest ministers, a peak of favor without parallel.

sentences.zh: 然進不由道，位過其任，莫能有終，所謂愛之適足以害之者也。

translations.literal: Yet advancement did not follow the Way, rank exceeded their charge, and none could finish well—what is called loving them was just enough to harm them.
translations.idiomatic: Yet they rose without moral right, outran their abilities, and none ended well—proof that doting favor can destroy what it cherishes.

sentences.zh: 漢世衰於元、成，壞於哀、平。

translations.literal: The Han declined from Yuan and Cheng and was broken from Ai and Ping.
translations.idiomatic: Han strength ebbed under Emperors Yuan and Cheng and shattered under Ai and Ping.

sentences.zh: 哀、平之際，國多釁矣。

translations.literal: At the turn of Ai and Ping, the state bore many cracks.
translations.idiomatic: Between those two reigns the dynasty was riven with fault lines.

sentences.zh: 主疾無嗣，弄臣為輔，鼎足不強，棟干微撓。

translations.literal: The ruler was ill without heir; plaything ministers served as props; the tripod's legs were not strong; the ridgepole and rafters bent slightly.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ailing and heirless, kept his balance on favorites; the "three legs" of state wobbled and the roof-tree swayed.

sentences.zh: 一朝帝崩，奸臣擅命，董賢縊死，丁、傅流放，辜及母后，奪位幽廢，咎在親便嬖，所任非仁賢。

translations.literal: One morning the emperor collapsed; treacherous ministers seized the mandate; Dong Xian was forced to hang himself; Ding and Fu were banished; guilt reached the empress dowager; the throne was wrested away and she was confined and deposed—the fault lay in trusting bedside favorites and employing men who were not benevolent worthies.
translations.idiomatic: When Ai died in a night, a usurper seized the brush; Dong Xian died on a rope, the Ding and Fu clans were swept into exile, and even the dowager paid for misrule—the price of filling office from the pillow instead of from virtue.

sentences.zh: 故仲尼著「損者三友」，王者不私人以官，殆為此也。

translations.literal: Therefore Zhongni recorded "the three kinds of friends who cause harm"; the true king does not hand out posts from private affection—it was roughly for this.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius therefore warned of "three friendships that injure you," and the sage-kings never handed out rank for love alone—this is what he meant."
